observations.summary: Father Aviel, once a sharp scholar, emotionally ceased functioning after the death of his wife, Mother Zuna, symbolizing a profound personal loss and internal break.

evidence.quote: But when Mother Zuna passed beyond recall—when they buried his bride beneath the earth—something stopped within him. Not died, which would have been cleaner and quicker, but simply ceased like a cart with a cruelly shattered axle.

observations.notes: Strong metaphor of emotional paralysis after loss.

observations.summary: Father Aviel discarded most of the gods he carried, keeping only one deity representing limitless dust and earthly plenty, who whispered that hoarded treasures might hold back time and defy its demands.

evidence.quote: So he swept them away and kept only one: the lord of limitless dust and earthly plenty, who whispered in the watches and the waiting of the night that hoarded treasures might hold back time itself and defy the demands it made.

observations.notes: Indicates a shift in spiritual focus and possible theme of resisting time or loss.
